name: Puerto Aralla, Collado Collarriondo y Hayedo de la Boyariza – circular desde Cubillas de Arbás (Reservas de la Biosfera del Alto Bernesga)
region_intro: Mountain biking around Villamanín, located in the province of León, Spain, offers diverse natural landscapes for outdoor activities. The region is characterized by mountainous terrain, deep gorges, and extensive forests, providing a varied backdrop for mountain bike trails. Significant elevation changes are common, making for challenging and rewarding rides across its natural features. Best mountain bike trails around Villamanín The most popular mountain bike trail is Puerto Aralla, Collado Collarriondo y Hayedo de la Boyariza – circular desde Cubillas de Arbás (Reservas de la Biosfera del Alto Bernesga), a difficult 21.7 miles (34.9 km) trail that takes 3 hours 49 minutes to complete. This route demands a high level of fitness and experience within the Alto Bernesga Biosphere Reserve. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Cruz de San Salvador y Cuito Negro – circular desde el Embalse de Casares por el Alto Bernesga, a difficult 22.6 miles (36.3 km) path. This challenging loop features significant elevation gain and requires good physical condition. Local mountain bikers also love the Collada Gistreo (1,634 m) – Cueto Negro Summit loop from Casares de Arbás, a 25.5 miles (41.1 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain with views towards Cueto Negro Summit, often completed in about 3 hours 16 minutes. Mountain biking around Villamanín is defined by mountainous terrain, gorges, and forests within the Alto Bernesga Biosphere Reserve. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Villamanín are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 16 reviews. More than 260 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Villamanín's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Villamanín?
A: There are over 15 mountain bike trails around Villamanín, offering a range of options for different skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Villamanín?
A: Mountain bike trails in Villamanín feature diverse natural landscapes, including mountainous terrain, deep gorges, and extensive forests. You can expect significant elevation changes, making for challenging and rewarding rides across varied natural features within the Alto Bernesga Biosphere Reserve.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Villamanín?
A: While many routes are challenging, there is at least one easy mountain bike trail available in Villamanín, offering a gentler introduction to the region's beautiful scenery.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Villamanín?
A: The region is generally suitable for mountain biking across various seasons, but spring and autumn often offer the most pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Some areas, like Los Calderones Gorge, can become impassable during periods of heavy rain, so checking local conditions is advisable.

Q: Are there any scenic viewpoints or natural features to see along the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, Villamanín is rich in natural beauty. You can find stunning features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4073598" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Calderones Gorge&lt;/a&gt;, the spectacular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3541942" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vegacervera Gorge&lt;/a&gt;, and the magical &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5235085" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ciñera Beech Forest&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes also offer views towards prominent peaks like Cueto Negro Summit.

Q: Can I find waterfalls on mountain bike routes near Villamanín?
A: Yes, the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5235085" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada de Piedrafita&lt;/a&gt; is located near the village of Piedrafita. While not directly on a mountain bike trail, it's a worthwhile natural spectacle accessible by a short walk from nearby routes.

Q: Are there family-friendly mountain bike routes in Villamanín?
A: For families, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5235085" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ciñera Beech Forest&lt;/a&gt; offers an easier area to explore. While most mountain bike trails in the region involve significant elevation changes, there is at least one easy route available that could be suitable for families looking for a less strenuous ride.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy the most about mountain biking in Villamanín?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 16 reviews. Mountain bikers often praise the diverse terrain, challenging climbs, and the stunning natural scenery found within the Alto Bernesga Biosphere Reserve.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes available?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike trails in Villamanín are circular. For example, you can explore the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1037722733/puerto-aralla-collado-collarriondo-y-hayedo-de-la-boyariza-circular-desde-cubillas-de-arbas-reservas-de-la-biosfera-del-alto-bernesga" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto Aralla, Collado Collarriondo y Hayedo de la Boyariza – circular desde Cubillas de Arbás&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20158912" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collada Gistreo (1,634 m) – Cueto Negro Summit loop from Casares de Arbás&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails in Villamanín?
A: While specific regulations for dogs on mountain bike trails can vary, the natural environment of Villamanín, particularly within the Alto Bernesga Biosphere Reserve, generally welcomes responsible dog owners. Always ensure your dog is under control and check for any local signage regarding protected areas or wildlife.

Q: Are there any particularly challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. Experienced riders seeking a challenge can tackle routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1040438388/cruz-de-san-salvador-y-cuito-negro-circular-desde-el-embalse-de-casares-por-el-alto-bernesga" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cruz de San Salvador y Cuito Negro – circular desde el Embalse de Casares por el Alto Bernesga&lt;/a&gt;, which features a substantial 1,410m elevation gain over 36.3 km. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20158895" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collada Gistreo (1,634 m) – View of Las Tres Marías loop from Villamanín&lt;/a&gt;, a 45 km route with over 1,300m of ascent.

Q: Is public transport available to access mountain bike trails in Villamanín?
A: Information on specific public transport links directly to trailheads is limited. However, Villamanín is a regional hub, and local bus services might connect to nearby villages. It's advisable to check local transport schedules if you plan to rely on public transport for trail access.
tips: Nice panoramic rolling along the Camino de la Cuencha with views of the town of Cubillas de Arbás and Pico La Brazosa standing out in the background.
A good moment to stop and take the opportunity to recover energy while we enjoy the views of the track that leads us to Puerto de Aralla.

Beautiful picture while we roll along the track that leaves Puerto Aralla (1,536 masl). The track runs halfway up the slope with beautiful views of the Sierra Peña La Cueva. An ideal place for mountain bike lovers.
If we roll in the direction of Puerto Aralla, we will have a magnificent panoramic view of Pico del Castro with its antennas.
Technical section (downhill) that takes us to the Casares reservoir along the "La Gril" route
